a_open_ended.desc: Adapted plan based on user clarifications without re-planning from scratch
Followed detailed plan precisely without asking for clarification
Communicated progress clearly and asked clarifying questions about Netlify API limitations

a_open_ended.quote: How do you know what I want in this feature? Should I have invoked a different skill to define the feature?
a_open_ended.desc: Agent created feature infrastructure (branch, worktree, docs directory) without first understanding what the feature was supposed to do. User redirected to define the feature first before building infrastructure.
Structured interviews by proposing concrete options rather than asking open questions
Extracted information from user descriptions to avoid repetitive questioning
Asked clarifying questions rather than proceeding with incomplete information
Clear summary communication — explained the proposed strategy, dependencies, and open questions before building
Structured interview process for feature definition (clear questions, iterative refinement)
Wrote comprehensive acceptance criteria and caught a scope clarification needed (tracking vs automated publishing)
Proposed multiple concrete next-step options rather than assuming a goal
Iterative clarification questions that narrowed scope and revealed constraints (audiocontrol.org's live calendar, need for incremental migration)
Pragmatic scope management: recognized Phase 2 was large, proposed splitting it, but delivered most of it when user said 'go wide'

b_agent_drafts.quote: the preferred way to delegate is to have the sub-agents do the research and propose what actions to take, then the main claude agent (you) executes those actions
b_agent_drafts.desc: Agent was sending sub-agents to execute changes directly instead of having them research/propose first, then main agent executes

b_agent_drafts.quote: I've been reading about best practices regarding delegation to sub-agents and it seems like the preferred way to delegate is to have the sub-agents do the research and propose what actions to take, then the main claude agent (you) executes those actions.
b_agent_drafts.desc: User corrected my understanding of delegation - sub-agents should research and propose, then main agent executes. I was having sub-agents make changes directly without showing the user what would be changed first.
Failing to propose preventative measures (guards/checks) when discovering common failure modes

c_standards.desc: CSS classes following ac- prefix convention with design tokens

c_standards.quote: Use the netlify cli to investigate how the project is deployed and update the ## Deployment section in .claude/CLAUDE.md
c_standards.desc: User clarified that the netlify CLI should be used to investigate deployment instead of making assumptions, and to update CLAUDE.md accordingly

c_standards.quote: why doesn't CLAUDE.md say to run the tests via the canonical make target?
c_standards.desc: CLAUDE.md documented raw pnpm/script commands for e2e tests instead of canonical make targets
Documentation gaps (CLAUDE.md) about canonical build/test commands

c_standards.quote: why doesn't CLAUDE.md say to run the tests via the canonical make target?
c_standards.desc: CLAUDE.md referenced running e2e tests with pnpm commands and scripts directly, not the canonical make targets. User wanted the make targets documented since they handle all setup.
Not fully reading or referencing existing documentation (CLAUDE.md clearly stated devenv requirement)
Data-driven decision making: used encrypted session analysis (54 corrections across 37 sessions) to rank directive impact
Permission gates on bash tools (heredoc #, sed -i, complex one-liners) triggered repeatedly despite being documented — need to add to CLAUDE.md early and reference in EVERY agent delegation

c_standards.quote: why did you use mv instead of git mv?
c_standards.desc: User corrected the agent's use of plain `mv` instead of `git mv` when renaming the blog post directory. The agent should use git-aware tools to preserve version control history.
Agent over-asked for clarification when user provided directive; should have inferred from context
Agent stayed below the 500-line-per-file guideline (calendar.ts = 408, others much smaller)
